title: Explorer Content Expansion Sweep
goal: Review the newly expanded curated catalog and the stronger topic-guide layer as a learning product, not only as a functional interface.
intent: Judge whether the added problems and topic materials make the explorer meaningfully more useful, more teachable, and still coherent under real browsing.
reviewQuestions: Do the new problems feel intentionally chosen or merely appended?
Does the detail pane surface the new learning material in a way that is actually worth opening?
Do the new topic pages read like pattern guides instead of filler content?
reminders: Do not stop at 'the new item exists'; inspect whether it is framed well enough to deserve space in the product.
When a detail pane looks correct, still scan nearby metadata density, spacing, and reading flow.
If a new topic page technically renders but still feels thin or generic, record that as a real failure.
baseline: Run the stable acceptance suite first.
Use the screenshots as evidence for both content quality and layout quality.
Write observations in product terms, not only in DOM or selector terms.

steps.title: Landing with expanded catalog
steps.intent: Check whether a larger curated set still feels selective rather than bloated.
steps.focus: Assess first-load hierarchy after the problem count increase.
lookAround: Does the catalog still feel curated with 71 items visible in the system?
Do the recommendations still read like picks rather than like an overloaded feed?
Does the top-level guide/navigation strip still feel balanced?
reviewQuestions: Would I still trust the curation after seeing the expanded scope?
steps.observation: The expanded catalog still needs to read like a field guide, not a dump.

steps.title: Inspect a newer linked-list medium
steps.intent: Verify that the new linked-list additions feel editor-reviewed and well-supported.
steps.focus: Review the detail framing for Merge Nodes in Between Zeros.
lookAround: Does the recommendation copy explain why this belongs in the collection?
Is the learning-support block actually useful, not just another list of links?
Does the row still feel distinct from neighboring classics?
reviewQuestions: Would this page help me decide to solve this problem next?
steps.observation: The new linked-list problem should feel like a deliberate modern addition rather than a random later-number append.

steps.title: Inspect a graph counting medium
steps.intent: Check whether graph additions improve range and still feel approachable.
steps.focus: Review Count Unreachable Pairs of Nodes in an Undirected Graph.

actions.value: Count Unreachable Pairs of Nodes in an Undirected Graph

lookAround: Does the graph/traversal framing stay readable?
Is the repo-support story still clear for a newer problem?
Do the nearby alternatives make sense, or do they feel mechanically chosen?
reviewQuestions: Does this make the graph section feel richer without becoming more intimidating?
steps.observation: The graph addition should broaden the curriculum without making the catalog feel noisier.

steps.title: Open the solution workspace
steps.intent: Check whether the new solution viewer feels attributable, readable, and worth using.
steps.focus: Review attributed implementations, language ordering, and inline annotations.
lookAround: Do the language tabs read in a sensible order?
Does attribution feel explicit and trustworthy?
Do the inline annotations help without turning the code view into noise?
reviewQuestions: Would I actually study from this solution surface instead of opening the repos manually?
steps.observation: The solution workspace should feel like a real study tool, not a pasted code dump.

steps.title: Search a prefix-sum addition and open the guide
steps.intent: Validate the new prefix-sums learning path from both the explorer and topic-page side.
steps.focus: Review Shifting Letters II and the new Prefix Sums And Difference Arrays guide.
lookAround: Does the narrow result still look intentional?
Does the detail pane now make the educational resource jump obvious enough to use?
If I click through to the guide afterward, does the topic page justify its existence?
reviewQuestions: Is the prefix-sums topic now a first-class part of the product?
steps.observation: The explorer should make the new prefix-sums route feel discoverable and worth following.

steps.title: Open the prefix-sums guide
steps.intent: Judge whether the new topic page has real teaching value.
steps.focus: Review the structure, fit signals, checklist, and linked resources on the Prefix Sums And Difference Arrays page.
lookAround: Does the page read like an educational reference or like generated filler?
Are the resource groups useful and clearly different from each other?
Do the related problems on the page make the topic feel grounded in the main explorer?
reviewQuestions: Would I open this page before or after solving, and would it help in either case?
steps.observation: The new topic page should feel like a genuine pattern guide, not just a placeholder for links.
